danger_signals.visual_form: Dark Guld's followers and clan members scattered across stages
Large enemy groups at end of each stage
danger_signals.meaning: Escalated danger requiring sustained combat effort

danger_signals.visual_form: Golden Axe enhancement during final boss fight
danger_signals.meaning: Heightened threat state; boss has amplified power
Territory under hostile control; widespread destruction indicates danger level

safety_signals.visual_form: Chicken Legs, Green Dragons, Fire Dragons available for mounting
safety_signals.meaning: Tactical advantage; enhanced combat capability and temporary invulnerability

safety_signals.visual_form: Movement from Ravaged Village through Castle to throne room
safety_signals.meaning: Advancement toward goal; structured path to victory

faction_indicators.faction: Player heroes (Ax Battler, Tyris Flare, Gilius Thunderhead)
faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Golden Axe (legendary weapon they seek to recover)
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Presence in Yuria; travel through destroyed lands to reach castle

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Golden Axe (corrupted possession as power source)
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Multiple follower types encountered progressively through stages
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Castle fortification; Ravaged Village and Ruins indicate territorial control; widespread devastation across Yuria

world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Backdrop context only; game begins after Dark Guld's release

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Ravaged Village, Ruins, destroyed environmental stages
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Continuous enemy spawning; escalating difficulty; hostile NPCs throughout

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Golden Axe wielded by boss; enhanced visual/behavioral intensity in final chamber
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Boss uses Golden Axe to enhance strength; longer battle duration; increased attack patterns
Game completion leads to 'new era of peace' narrative conclusion

environmental_storytelling.example: Ravaged Village and Ruins stages show aftermath of Dark Guld's conquest; visual decay indicates how recently/severely the land was attacked
Journey from destroyed outer lands → Tower → Dragon's Throat Cave → Castle Gates → Castle → Dark Guld's Chamber visualizes approach to seat of power
Boss encounters at stage ends feature 'large group of enemies at once,' indicating concentration of threat near Dark Guld's stronghold
Golden Axe possessed by Dark Guld signals corruption of legendary weapon; its recovery is visual end-goal

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Melee attacks with character-specific weapons; elemental magic usage at variable levels
Enhanced mobility and attack range; tactical advantage against grouped enemies
Uses Golden Axe enhancement; increased aggression; requires prolonged sustained combat
Variety of enemy types deployed; increasing difficulty; grouped encounters near castle

notes: Golden Axe II communicates world-state primarily through environmental destruction imagery (ravaged/ruined stages) and spatial progression toward the antagonist's seat of power. Danger is signaled through enemy density escalation and boss encounters. Faction identity relies on artifact possession (Golden Axe) and territorial markers rather than visual color-coding. The game uses level layout and enemy placement as narrative tools showing Dark Guld's control expanding outward from his castle. Character differentiation (dwarf/barbarian/amazon with unique magic types) establishes player faction through gameplay mechanics rather than visual markers.
